text: All candidates must provide one answer for each question from the choice of a or b. You therefore need to provide six answers in total.
Where appropriate (e.g. for formulae, equations, or diagrams) you may hand write or draw on the Inspera paper provided. You should not copy and paste images from any other documents.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Discuss how certain drugs modify peripheral neurotransmission by affecting the lifetime of the released neurotransmitters.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: It is essential to identify a valid target before embarking on a new drug discovery initiative – discuss.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Discuss with examples the common principles of combination therapy in the treatment of infectious diseases.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Discuss the mechanisms by which antibiotics inhibit protein synthesis.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Explain how epidermal growth factor (EGF) receptor signalling can cause cancer.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Compare and contrast the different types of targeted anti-cancer therapeutics.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Describe with examples how biologics can be used in treating inflammatory and autoimmune diseases.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Describe the mechanistic basis for using different drugs against asthma compared to Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD).

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: How can drugs be used to reduce myocardial ischaemia?

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Drugs often bind to receptors in one cell type but have consequences in other cells. Explain how this may occur in the cardiovascular system.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Discuss how Type 2 diabetes can be treated with small molecule drugs.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer ONE from either a or b for each question.
content: Describe with examples how drugs control neuronal excitability in central nervous system disorders.
